google.formatted_address: Corner of Lee &, Master St, Philadelphia, PA 19125, 1355 N Front St, Philadelphia, PA 19122, USA

samples.author: Rob Gambino
samples.text:
BEWARE BEWARE!  Unfair Cancellation Fee Experience

I recently had a very disappointing experience with this restaurant. I had to cancel my reservation because I tested positive for COVID-19 and was too sick to attend. I tried calling to explain the situation, but no one answered or returned my calls. Despite giving more than 24 hour + notice, I was still charged a cancellation fee for a dinner I never even attended.

For a so called michelin star omakase if they are not getting a fresh delivery every morning then everyone is eating DAY OLD fish EWWW!

It’s incredibly unfair to penalize someone under those circumstances, especially when there was legitimate notice and a valid health reason. I would have expected a more understanding and responsive approach, particularly given today’s environment. Unfortunately, this experience left a bad impression and I don’t plan on booking here again.

They didn't even call me back, they wont even return my email look at the images they just shoot t

samples.author: Siam Iamsakuldacha
samples.text:
We visited Hiroki for a special occasion. My wife had just given birth and, after nine months, was finally able to enjoy sushi again. The food was outstanding as always. It was fresh, beautifully prepared, and consistently high quality. The staff was also professional and attentive, just like the last time we were here.

One small note: One of the waitresses had a noticeable sniffle and sounded congested. She did not cough and made an effort to keep a reasonable distance, so I do not want to fault her. However, as a new parent, hearing someone continuously sniffling does make me nervous. If this comes up in the future, I would appreciate the staff considering simple precautions like offering or using a face mask to help guests feel more comfortable.

samples.author: susan weber
samples.text:
Last night we did the 21 piece seasonal omakase with sake and I'm so glad we did.  It was technically excellent, creative and fun.  World class.  An absolute must.

I hope the chef didn't mind that I couldn't eat the last few pieces.  Luckily, my dinner companion was thrilled to help!

Can't wait to come back. Thanks so much for great service and a memorable evening!

samples.author: Anthony Mobile
samples.text:
I went with my brother. It was a very quiet and intimate experience.

Service was fantastic and very educational.

The food was amazing and we left full after 2 hours of eating. Absolutely loved it!

We did the omakase plus sake pairing and both were amazing. There was no wait time at all

samples.author: Victor “Bud” Nye
samples.text: This was our first omakase dinner experience ever and we absolutely loved it. Watching the preparation was an art form in itself. Every dish was amazing and fresh and well thought out. An incredible and unique experience for sure. This was some of the best sushi we had ever had. The toro, the Black Sea bass, the sea eel, it was all just incredible. Do not skip this place if you like omakase.
